title: GASTAT Agricultural Statistics 2024 — Saudi Arabia
abstract: Annual national agricultural statistics report published by the General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT), covering crop area/production by administrative region (cereals, open-field and greenhouse vegetables, cut flowers, palm and other perennial trees), agricultural inputs (fertilizer, pesticides), organic agriculture, agricultural loans and bank credit, and land-use indicators. The workbook contains 53 sheets organized into 12 sections plus an index and summary.

variables.name: region
variables.label: Administrative Region
variables.description: One of Saudi Arabia's 13 administrative regions (Riyadh, Makkah, Madinah, Qassim, Eastern Region, Aseer, Tabuk, Hail, Northern Borders, Jazan, Najran, Al-Baha, Al-Jouf), or a Kingdom total row.

variables.name: crop_or_item
variables.label: Crop / Commodity
variables.description: Crop or commodity name (e.g. wheat, alfalfa, dates, olives), varies by sheet/section.

variables.name: harvested_area
variables.label: Harvested Area
variables.description: Cultivated/harvested area for the crop and region.

variables.name: production_quantity
variables.label: Production Quantity
variables.description: Reported production quantity for the crop and region.

access.how_to_request: Published openly by GASTAT on the national statistics portal; no request required. Local copy available on request from the KAUST Data Inventory Team.

provenance.originator: General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT), Saudi Arabia
provenance.citation: General Authority for Statistics (GASTAT). 2024. Agricultural Statistics 2024. Riyadh: GASTAT.

known_issues: No fisheries or aquaculture section is present in this workbook despite being catalogued under the Production domain via the KAUST Sources registry — all 12 sections cover crop agriculture (cereals, vegetables, cut flowers, palm/perennial trees), inputs, organic farming, agricultural credit, and land-use indicators. Included here because it appears as a local file referenced by the team's Sources doc; consider re-scoping or excluding from seafood-production analyses.
n_rows (1,100) is a rough estimate based on typical per-sheet row counts (~20 rows x ~51 data sheets); the workbook was not fully parsed sheet-by-sheet, so the true total is uncertain.
Only a single edition (2024) is present locally; no historical time series was found for this file.
